text:
MEDICAL ONCOLOGY FOLLOW-UP NOTE

Patient: Diane Castellano
Age: 56 years
Visit: Routine follow-up, nivolumab (Opdivo) maintenance

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT
Routine oncology follow-up. Patient presenting for interval assessment on Opdivo maintenance therapy. Approximately 20 months from initial diagnosis of stage IV melanoma.

---

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS
Ms. Castellano is a 56-year-old female landscape architect from the southwestern US with a history of BRAF V600E-mutant stage IV nodular melanoma, initially diagnosed approximately 20 months ago. Her disease course has been notable for several treatment phases:

She was initially treated with dabrafenib/trametinib (dab/tram), achieving a partial response at ~3 months with subsequent stabilization. At 8 months she developed disease progression including new brain metastases. She underwent stereotactic radiosurgery (SRS) to the brain lesions followed by initiation of ipilimumab/nivolumab (ipi/nivo) combination immunotherapy. Her course was complicated by immune-mediated colitis requiring hospitalization after cycle 1, managed with high-dose steroids; she recovered well without long-term sequelae. She subsequently transitioned to Opdivo maintenance monotherapy. At 13 months from diagnosis, she was noted to be responding and developed immune-mediated hypothyroidism, started on levothyroxine. Imaging at 15 months confirmed continued response. Most recent imaging (obtained prior to today's visit) showed a deep partial response with residual hepatic lesion measuring approximately 0.9 cm, representing marked interval improvement from prior.

Today she presents for her interval office visit. She reports feeling well overall. She has returned to full-time landscape architecture work and is managing multiple design projects. She notes occasional fatigue toward the end of the week, which she attributes to her work schedule rather than any medication side effects. No abdominal pain, no diarrhea, no blood in stool — she is particularly aware of GI symptoms given her prior colitis history. Appetite is good. No fevers. No new skin changes. No headaches. No focal neurological symptoms.

Levothyroxine 75 mcg daily — she is taking this consistently in the morning on an empty stomach, as instructed. No symptoms suggestive of hypothyroidism or hyperthyroidism.

She received her last Opdivo infusion approximately 4 weeks ago without difficulty. No infusion reactions reported.

---

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS
Constitutional: No fevers, no significant night sweats. Mild end-of-week fatigue, functional. Weight stable.
Neurological: No headaches, no vision changes, no focal weakness or numbness. No seizures. Performing complex cognitive work (landscape design, client consultations) without difficulty.
Cardiovascular: No chest pain, no palpitations, no dyspnea on exertion.
Respiratory: No cough, no shortness of breath.
GI: No nausea, no vomiting, no diarrhea, no constipation. No abdominal cramping. No rectal bleeding. Bowel habits at her personal baseline (~1x/day, formed).
GU: No dysuria. Menopause confirmed, no new symptoms.
Skin: No new rashes, no pruritus. No vitiligo progression noted by patient (mild depigmentation on forearms noted previously, stable).
Musculoskeletal: No joint pain, no myalgias. Full range of motion.
Endocrine: No cold/heat intolerance, no hair changes. On levothyroxine.
Psychiatric: Mood good. She describes herself as "cautiously optimistic." No depression or anxiety symptoms reported.

---

PAST ONCOLOGIC HISTORY
- Stage IV BRAF V600E+ nodular melanoma, dx ~20 months ago
- Systemic therapy: dab/tram x ~8 months (PR then stabilization then progression)
- SRS to brain metastases (exact number and location per radiation oncology records)
- Ipi/nivo x 1 cycle (complicated by grade 3 immune-mediated colitis, hospitalized)
- Opdivo maintenance monotherapy — ongoing, currently ~9 months of maintenance
- irAEs: pyrexia on dab/tram; colitis on ipi/nivo (grade 3, steroid-responsive); hypothyroidism on nivo (grade 1-2, on replacement)

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY
- Hypothyroidism (immune-related, as above)
- No other significant PMHx prior to oncologic diagnosis

MEDICATIONS
- Nivolumab (Opdivo) 480 mg IV q4 weeks
- Levothyroxine 75 mcg PO qAM

ALLERGIES
- No known drug allergies

SOCIAL HISTORY
- Landscape architect, full-time (recently returned to full-time work)
- Southwestern US
- Non-smoker
- Alcohol: occasional, social
- Lives with partner; strong support system

FAMILY HISTORY
- Non-contributory per chart

---

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION
General: Well-appearing female, appears stated age or younger. NAD. Pleasant and engaged. Well-nourished.
Vital signs: BP within normal limits, HR regular, afebrile, SpO2 normal on room air. Weight stable compared to last visit.
ECOG performance status: 0
Skin: Mild vitiliginous depigmentation on bilateral forearms, stable. No new rashes. No acneiform eruption. No skin breakdown. No suspicious pigmented lesions on visible skin surfaces.
HEENT: Sclerae anicteric. Mucous membranes moist. No lymphadenopathy in cervical, submandibular, or supraclavicular chains.
Chest/Pulmonary: Clear to auscultation bilaterally. No crackles, no wheeze.
Cardiovascular: RRR, normal S1/S2, no murmurs.
Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. No organomegaly appreciated. Normal bowel sounds. No guarding or rigidity — reassuring given colitis history.
Extremities: No peripheral edema. No clubbing.
Neurological: Alert and oriented x3. CN II-XII grossly intact. No focal deficits. Gait normal.

---

LAB RESULTS
CBC: WBC, hemoglobin, platelets all within normal limits.
CMP: LDH within normal range — notably reassuring. Liver enzymes (AST, ALT, alk phos) all normal, no hepatotoxicity signal. Creatinine normal.
TSH: Within normal therapeutic range on current levothyroxine dose. Free T4 normal. No dose adjustment needed at this time.
Cortisol/adrenal axis: Not rechecked today; no symptoms to suggest adrenal insufficiency.

---

IMAGING
Most recent cross-sectional imaging (CT chest/abdomen/pelvis, obtained prior to this visit, reviewed today): Continued deep partial response. The previously identified hepatic lesion has decreased further to approximately 0.9 cm. No new sites of disease. Lungs clear. No new lymphadenopathy. Brain MRI (most recent, reviewed): No new intracranial metastases. SRS treatment sites without concerning change.

---

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN

1. Stage IV BRAF V600E+ melanoma — durable deep partial response on Opdivo maintenance
Ms. Castellano continues to do exceptionally well. She is approximately 20 months from her initial diagnosis, with an ongoing and deepening response to nivolumab. The hepatic lesion has progressively shrunk to 0.9 cm on most recent imaging. LDH is normal. Performance status is ECOG 0. This represents a very gratifying response, particularly given the complexity of her earlier course (BRAF-targeted therapy, CNS disease, colitis). We discussed her trajectory at length today.

Treatment duration: Raised the question of whether to continue Opdivo maintenance indefinitely versus considering a planned treatment-free interval. There is emerging clinical evidence (including data from melanoma-specific cohorts) supporting that patients with durable deep responses may do well with a planned hold after 2 years of total immunotherapy. Her ipi/nivo cycle counts as the start of her immunotherapy — she is currently ~11 months into Opdivo monotherapy, and approximately 11-12 months of total immunotherapy duration. We discussed that the decision point is approaching (anticipating approximately 24-month total immunotherapy mark). I explained the rationale and the uncertainty: durable responses off therapy have been observed, but recurrence risk remains. She asked thoughtful questions and expressed openness to both continued treatment and a treatment holiday. We will formally revisit this discussion at the 24-month mark and will consider input from our multidisciplinary tumor board at that time.

Continue Opdivo 480 mg IV q4 weeks in the interim. Repeat imaging in approximately 4 months (i.e., ~24 months from diagnosis) to reassess.

2. Immune-mediated hypothyroidism — stable on levothyroxine 75 mcg
TSH and free T4 in appropriate range. No dose change needed. Continue current dose. Recheck TFTs in 3 months or at next visit.

3. Prior immune-mediated colitis — resolved, no recurrence
No GI symptoms today. Exam benign. Counseled to continue vigilance and to call immediately with any return of diarrhea, cramping, or blood in stool. No prophylactic measures needed at this time.

4. Brain metastases — post-SRS, no new CNS disease
Most recent brain MRI reassuring. No new lesions, no leptomeningeal disease. Will continue to follow with periodic MRI per standard intervals. No neurosurgical or radiation oncology follow-up needed at present per recent communications.

5. Quality of life and functional status
This was a meaningful part of today's visit. Ms. Castellano has returned to full-time work as a landscape architect, which is a role she is deeply invested in. She describes her quality of life as subjectively very good. We discussed the importance of sun protection given her history — she works outdoors regularly, and I reinforced daily broad-spectrum SPF 50+ sunscreen, sun-protective clothing, and avoidance of peak UV hours where feasible given her occupational demands. She is engaged with this and asked about specific product recommendations. Discussed skin surveillance — referred for annual full-body derm check if not already in place.

Return to clinic: 4 weeks for next Opdivo infusion per standard schedule. Office visit in approximately 3 months for interval clinical assessment and labs. Sooner if any concerns.

--- Electronically signed ---
